events.event_description: First U.S. chief of mission (Chargé d'Affaires John Bozman Kerr) presented credentials to Nicaragua, establishing the U.S. legation.

evidence.quote: John Bozman Kerr, Chargé d'Affaires. Appointed: 1851-03-12. Credentials presented: 1852-02-18.

events.event_description: Legation Managua was formally closed by Chargé d'Affaires ad interim John Hanaford Gregory.

evidence.quote: John Hanaford Gregory, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Notified the Government of Nicaragua of the closing of Legation Managua on 1909-03-12.

events.event_description: Legation Managua reopened when Elliott Northcott presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: Elliott Northcott, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1911-01-09. Credentials presented: 1911-02-21. Left post on 1911-06-23.

events.event_description: Legation elevated to Embassy when James Bolton Stewart was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presented credentials.

evidence.quote: James Bolton Stewart, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1942-03-05. Credentials presented: 1942-06-12. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1943-04-14. James Bolton Stewart, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1943-03-27. Credentials presented: 1943-04-14.
